title: ADR: Remediate Failing Upload Workflow
context: Users are reporting upload failures, but the specific workflow, surface, and failure mode are entirely unknown.
The current state cannot be characterized beyond user complaints; there is no baseline success rate, no identified storage backend, and no confirmed auth model.
Constraints require that data integrity be preserved for all uploaded content.
decision: We will remediate the identified upload workflow after resolving all six open questions, because the intake lacks sufficient specificity to prescribe a technical approach — the affected surface, failure mode, storage backend, and auth model are all unknown. Until those questions are answered, decomposition must be blocked.

inScope: The specific upload workflow confirmed as failing once identified
Restoration of upload success rate to the pre-complaint baseline
Validation that the identified failure mode no longer occurs
outOfScope: Upload workflows not implicated in the reported complaints
Redesign of the upload architecture or storage backend
Performance optimization of uploads not related to the reported failure

validationPlan.ac: The identified upload workflow completes successfully under normal operating conditions.
validationPlan.verification: Execute the confirmed failing upload workflow end-to-end and observe successful completion.

validationPlan.ac: Users no longer encounter the upload failure that prompted complaints.
validationPlan.verification: Monitor complaint volume and upload success rate after deployment.

risks.risk: Fix targets the wrong workflow, leaving the actual failure unresolved
risks.trigger: Open questions answered incompletely before decomposition
risks.mitigation: Require written confirmation from owners on all open questions
risks.detection: Post-deploy monitoring still shows failure signals

risks.trigger: Fix modifies upload handling without accounting for partial-write behavior
risks.mitigation: Audit storage backend for orphaned artifacts during investigation
risks.detection: Discrepancy between upload attempts and persisted artifacts

risks.risk: Root cause spans multiple workflows, making a single fix insufficient
risks.trigger: Failure is systemic rather than isolated to one upload path
risks.mitigation: Check whether failure affects more than one surface before scoping the fix
risks.detection: New complaints surface for a different workflow after fix

alternatives.name: Broad defensive hardening of all upload paths
alternatives.reasonRejected: Speculative without knowing which path is failing; risks introducing regressions.

alternatives.name: Rollback to the last known-good deployment
alternatives.reasonRejected: Assumes failure was introduced by a recent code change, which is unconfirmed.

openQuestions.question: Which specific upload workflow or feature is failing?
What surface is affected — web, mobile, or API?
What error messages or failure modes are users observing?
What is the auth model — are uploads restricted to authenticated users?
What data source or storage backend handles uploads?
What is the current upload success rate and the expected baseline?
